title: AZ-900 Mock Exam 3: Architecture, Security, and Governance
description: A 50-question unofficial mock paper aligned to the AZ-900 skills measured from July 20, 2026. Uses realistic dropdown, statement, single-choice, and multiple-answer formats with original questions and official Microsoft Learn references.

items.prompt: {{blank}} owns and operates the physical servers and facilities in a public cloud.
items.explanation: The public-cloud provider owns and maintains the shared physical infrastructure and offers services to customers. Customers manage their own data, identities, configuration, and other responsibilities according to the service model.

items.prompt: Evaluate each statement about cloud deployment models.

statements.text: A private cloud is dedicated to one organization
A public cloud is operated by a provider for its customers
A hybrid cloud must contain only public-cloud resources

items.explanation: Private cloud infrastructure is dedicated to one organization, while a provider operates public-cloud facilities for customers. Hybrid designs combine environments, so they are not restricted to public resources.

items.prompt: A team moves an application from customer-owned servers to Azure virtual machines. Which TWO financial or responsibility changes should it expect?
answers: Physical host maintenance transfers to Microsoft
Eligible resource charges can follow consumption
options: Physical host maintenance transfers to Microsoft
Eligible resource charges can follow consumption
Microsoft becomes responsible for the application’s user accounts
The virtual machines become customer-owned datacenter hardware
items.explanation: Azure operates the physical hosts and meters cloud resources, but the customer still manages guest configuration, application identities, and data. Consuming a VM does not transfer ownership of Azure hardware.

items.prompt: A research organization alone uses a cloud environment hosted on dedicated infrastructure. Which deployment model is described?
items.explanation: A private cloud serves one organization on dedicated cloud infrastructure, whether hosted on-premises or by another party. No second environment is described, and SaaS is an application service type.

items.prompt: A web API runs on Azure App Service. Who is responsible for fixing a vulnerability in the API’s own code?
items.answer: The customer that develops the API
options: The customer that develops the API
Microsoft because App Service is PaaS
items.explanation: PaaS transfers operation of the host platform to Microsoft but not responsibility for customer application code and data. The development team must remediate vulnerabilities in what it builds.

items.prompt: Adding more virtual machine instances behind a load balancer is an example of scaling {{blank}}.
items.explanation: Horizontal or scale-out changes add instances so work can be distributed. Vertical scaling enlarges an existing instance, and the other choices are storage or deployment changes rather than scale directions.

references.title: Describe the benefits of using cloud services

items.prompt: Evaluate each statement about availability, reliability, and governance.

statements.text: High availability focuses on keeping a service accessible
Reliability can use redundancy and recovery to tolerate failures
Governance removes the need for all security controls

items.explanation: Availability and reliability use resilient design, redundancy, and recovery to meet service goals despite failures. Governance supplies oversight but complements rather than replaces security controls.

references.title: Describe the benefits of using cloud services

items.prompt: A business wants to forecast both expected cloud cost and application performance before a release. Which cloud benefit is most relevant?
items.explanation: Predictability uses pricing models, monitoring, and performance evidence to improve cost and performance forecasts. The other choices describe isolation, assets, or information governance.

references.title: Describe the benefits of using cloud services

items.prompt: A platform restores service in another location after a regional failure. Which cloud benefit does that recovery capability primarily demonstrate?
items.explanation: Reliability includes recovering from failures and continuing to deliver intended function. Authentication and billing are unrelated, and manual management is not the core outcome of regional recovery.

references.title: Describe the benefits of using cloud services

items.prompt: {{blank}} gives a customer the most control over guest operating systems and networking.
items.explanation: IaaS exposes virtualized infrastructure and leaves guest operating systems and much network configuration under customer management. PaaS and SaaS progressively shift more of the stack to the provider.

items.prompt: For each scenario, decide whether it describes software as a service (SaaS).

statements.text: Employees use Microsoft 365 without managing its application servers
The provider manages the application servers for a complete subscription product
Developers deploy their own code to a provider-managed runtime

items.explanation: A complete provider-operated application is SaaS, as illustrated by Microsoft 365 and other subscription products. Deploying customer code to a managed runtime instead describes PaaS.

items.prompt: Which TWO statements describe the platform-as-a-service responsibility model?
answers: The provider patches the operating system and managed runtime
The customer manages its application code and data
options: The provider patches the operating system and managed runtime
The customer manages its application code and data
The customer replaces failed physical disks
The provider defines all customer business data classifications
items.explanation: PaaS lets the provider operate infrastructure and platform software while the customer owns the deployed application and its information. Physical repairs are provider tasks in all cloud models, but data classification remains with the customer.

items.prompt: A company subscribes to a provider-managed payroll product and configures only users and business settings. Which service type is this?
items.explanation: A finished payroll product consumed by subscription is SaaS. The customer does not operate infrastructure or deploy its own application onto a managed development platform.

items.prompt: An administrator must install a custom network appliance that requires guest operating-system access. Which model is appropriate?
items.explanation: IaaS gives the customer administrative control of a virtual machine guest OS, which is necessary for the appliance. Managed platforms and finished SaaS applications do not expose that level of host control.

items.prompt: A geographic area containing one or more Azure datacenters is an Azure {{blank}}.
items.explanation: An Azure region is a geographic deployment area containing datacenter infrastructure connected by a low-latency network. Resource and management groups are logical scopes, and an availability set is a VM resiliency construct.

items.prompt: Evaluate each statement about Azure scopes.

statements.text: A subscription can contain multiple resource groups
A resource group belongs to one subscription
An availability zone can contain subscriptions for billing

items.explanation: Subscriptions contain resource groups, and each resource group belongs to one subscription. Availability zones are physical fault domains inside regions, not logical billing containers.

items.prompt: An administrator moves a supported resource to another resource group in the same subscription. Does this action automatically change its Azure region?
items.answer: No, changing the resource group does not itself relocate the resource
options: No, changing the resource group does not itself relocate the resource
Yes, it moves to the resource group’s metadata region
Yes, it moves to the nearest region pair
No, because Azure resources never have a region
items.explanation: Resource-group membership is a logical management relationship, not a data-plane relocation instruction. Regional movement requires a service-specific migration or redeployment where supported.

items.prompt: A resource group is deleted successfully. What normally happens to the resources contained by it?
items.answer: The contained resources are deleted with the resource group
options: The contained resources are deleted with the resource group
They move to the subscription root
They become members of every other resource group
They are converted into management groups
items.explanation: A resource group is a lifecycle boundary; deleting it requests deletion of its contained resources. This is why related resources are commonly grouped and deletion must be treated as consequential.

items.prompt: Use Azure {{blank}} when an on-premises network needs a private dedicated connection to Microsoft cloud services.
options: VPN Gateway over the public internet
items.explanation: ExpressRoute uses a connectivity provider to establish private connectivity that does not traverse the public internet. VPN Gateway uses encrypted internet tunnels, while DNS and Policy solve different needs.

references.title: Describe Azure compute and networking services

items.prompt: Evaluate each statement about Azure compute choices.

statements.text: Azure Functions can run code in response to events
Azure Virtual Desktop is a DNS hosting service
Azure App Service requires customers to patch its physical servers

items.explanation: Functions supplies event-driven serverless compute, and App Service is a provider-managed platform. Azure Virtual Desktop delivers Windows desktops and applications rather than authoritative DNS hosting.

references.title: Describe Azure compute and networking services

items.prompt: Which TWO requirements point to Azure Virtual Machines rather than a managed web or serverless platform?
answers: Administrator access to the guest operating system
Installation of legacy server software with custom dependencies
options: Administrator access to the guest operating system
Installation of legacy server software with custom dependencies
Automatic execution from an event with no server management
A complete hosted email product for end users
items.explanation: Virtual machines fit workloads that require OS-level control and unusual server dependencies. Event-only code is a Functions scenario, while a complete hosted product is SaaS rather than customer-managed compute.

references.title: Describe Azure compute and networking services

items.prompt: Which service hosts public or private DNS zones and answers name-resolution queries?
items.explanation: Azure DNS hosts DNS domains on Azure name servers and resolves record queries. Advisor, Files, and Policy provide recommendations, storage, and governance instead of name resolution.

references.title: Describe Azure compute and networking services

items.prompt: A team wants a managed Kubernetes control plane for orchestrating many containerized services. Which service should it select?
items.explanation: AKS provides managed Kubernetes orchestration for scheduling, scaling, and operating container workloads. Container Instances runs containers without a full Kubernetes control plane, and the other services are unrelated.

references.title: Describe Azure compute and networking services

items.prompt: An Azure virtual machine uses a managed {{blank}} for durable block storage.
items.explanation: Azure managed disks provide block storage volumes for virtual machines. Queues hold messages, Tables hold key-value entities, and a DNS zone contains name records.

items.prompt: Evaluate each storage-service match.

statements.text: Azure Files provides managed file shares
Queue Storage is designed to attach an operating-system disk to a VM
Table Storage is a relational SQL database service

items.explanation: Azure Files exposes file shares. Managed disks back VM volumes, Queue Storage holds messages, and Table Storage is a schemaless NoSQL key-value service rather than a relational database.

items.prompt: Which TWO statements about locally redundant storage (LRS) are correct?
answers: Its copies remain within one physical datacenter in the primary region
options: Its copies remain within one physical datacenter in the primary region
It replicates to a secondary region
It distributes copies across availability zones
items.explanation: LRS protects against local disk and rack failures by keeping copies in one datacenter. Regional replication requires a geo option, and zonal distribution requires ZRS or GZRS.

items.prompt: A developer wants a graphical desktop tool to browse blobs, file shares, queues, and tables. Which tool should be used?
items.explanation: Storage Explorer provides a graphical interface for multiple Azure Storage data services. The alternatives report health, estimate costs, or manage hybrid infrastructure.

items.prompt: An on-premises Windows file server should cache an Azure file share and synchronize changes between locations. Which service supports this?
items.explanation: Azure File Sync centralizes shares in Azure Files while Windows Servers cache frequently used data and synchronize changes. Data Box and Migrate support transfer projects, while lifecycle management changes blob tiers.

items.prompt: Authorization to create, read, update, or delete Azure resources is commonly assigned with Azure {{blank}}.
items.explanation: Azure RBAC maps identities to permitted management actions at a scope. Storage tiers affect price and retrieval, Service Health reports incidents, and Data Box transfers data.

references.title: Describe Azure identity, access, and security

items.prompt: Which TWO capabilities are provided by Microsoft Defender for Cloud?
items.explanation: Defender for Cloud assesses security posture and can provide workload protections for supported resources. DNS management and storage-tier selection belong to different Azure services.

references.title: Describe Azure identity, access, and security

items.prompt: A user signs in with a FIDO2 security key instead of a password. Which category describes the method?
items.explanation: A FIDO2 security key can authenticate a user without transmitting or entering a reusable password. It establishes identity; resource authorization remains a separate decision made after authentication.

references.title: Describe Azure identity, access, and security

items.prompt: Which Zero Trust principle assumes attackers may already be present and designs monitoring and segmentation accordingly?
items.explanation: Assume breach treats compromise as possible and limits blast radius through segmentation, monitoring, and response. Implicit trust, excessive privilege, and missing telemetry contradict Zero Trust.

references.title: Describe Azure identity, access, and security

items.prompt: A sign-in should be allowed only from compliant devices and should require MFA when risk is elevated. What should be configured?
items.explanation: Conditional Access combines identity, device, location, and risk signals with access controls. Storage lifecycle, managed domain features, and locks do not evaluate sign-in context.

references.title: Describe Azure identity, access, and security

items.prompt: Metadata such as CostCenter=42 used to group resources in reports is an Azure {{blank}}.
items.explanation: Tags are name-value metadata used to organize resources and allocate costs. They do not create physical isolation, private network connectivity, or protection from management changes.

items.prompt: Which TWO Cost Management capabilities help a team control monthly expenditure?
answers: Cost analysis grouped by service or tag
options: Cost analysis grouped by service or tag
items.explanation: Budgets alert against actual or forecast thresholds, and cost analysis exposes spending patterns for action. Zones improve resiliency and locks prevent changes; neither creates a financial discount.

items.prompt: Which Azure service can identify an underused virtual machine and recommend a smaller size to reduce cost?
items.explanation: Azure Advisor turns utilization analysis into optimization recommendations such as resizing. Raw metrics provide evidence but not the Advisor recommendation, while identity and file services are unrelated.

items.prompt: A team stops a virtual machine but leaves it allocated. What must it do to stop the VM compute charge?
items.explanation: Compute billing for a standard VM stops when the VM is deallocated, though attached storage and other resources can still incur charges. Metadata and locks do not change allocation state.

items.prompt: Evaluate each proposed governance control.

statements.text: Use Azure Policy to require a cost-center tag
Use Microsoft Purview to scale a virtual machine
Use a resource lock to assign a user the Reader role

items.explanation: Azure Policy can require or remediate resource metadata. Purview governs data rather than compute size, and locks constrain changes without granting any identity permission.

references.title: Describe Azure governance and compliance tools

items.prompt: Which TWO scopes can receive an Azure Policy assignment that then applies to resources beneath the scope?
items.explanation: Management groups and subscriptions are scopes in the Azure resource hierarchy and support policy assignment. Availability zones and racks are physical infrastructure concepts, not customer governance scopes.

references.title: Describe Azure governance and compliance tools

items.prompt: A critical database must be readable and manageable but protected from accidental deletion. Which feature is the narrowest match?
items.explanation: A delete lock blocks deletion while allowing authorized updates, which matches the stated requirement. A read-only lock would also block changes, and tags or endpoints do not prevent deletion.

references.title: Describe Azure governance and compliance tools

items.prompt: A policy assignment at a management group affects an existing child subscription. Why?
items.answer: Policy assignments inherit down the Azure resource hierarchy
options: Policy assignments inherit down the Azure resource hierarchy
The subscription becomes a resource group
A storage tier translates the rule
items.explanation: Azure governance assignments can inherit from management groups to child subscriptions, resource groups, and resources. Physical zones and storage configuration are unrelated to scope inheritance.

references.title: Describe Azure governance and compliance tools

items.prompt: Which pair of command environments can be selected within Azure Cloud Shell?
items.explanation: Cloud Shell offers Bash with Azure CLI or PowerShell with Azure PowerShell in an authenticated browser session. The other choices are transfer tools, services, or resource concepts rather than shell environments.

references.title: Describe tools for managing and deploying Azure resources

items.prompt: Why should an ARM or Bicep definition be stored in source control?
items.answer: To review, version, and repeat the declared infrastructure change
options: To review, version, and repeat the declared infrastructure change
To convert every workload to SaaS automatically
To guarantee that no resource can ever change
items.explanation: Source control records infrastructure changes and supports review and repeatable releases. It does not change service models, eliminate all drift, or replace the control plane that performs deployment.

references.title: Describe tools for managing and deploying Azure resources

items.prompt: A template contains a web app and its hosting plan. What is a key benefit of deploying them together?
items.answer: Dependencies and configuration can be applied consistently as one repeatable deployment
options: Dependencies and configuration can be applied consistently as one repeatable deployment
Both resources become free of charge
The template grants every user Owner access
The resources no longer use Azure Resource Manager
items.explanation: Declarative deployments coordinate related resources and dependencies consistently. Templates do not remove charges or authorization requirements, and Resource Manager remains the deployment control plane.

references.title: Describe tools for managing and deploying Azure resources

items.prompt: A team wants to manage Azure from a local program rather than a portal or shell. Which interface can it call?
items.answer: Azure Resource Manager REST APIs or an Azure SDK
options: Azure Resource Manager REST APIs or an Azure SDK
items.explanation: Azure exposes management operations through Resource Manager APIs and supported SDKs, using the same control plane as other tools. Hardware, storage tiers, and physical zones are not programming interfaces.

references.title: Describe tools for managing and deploying Azure resources

items.prompt: A developer edits a concise declarative file with symbolic resource names and then deploys it through Resource Manager. Which language is likely being used?
items.explanation: Bicep is Azure’s concise declarative infrastructure language and compiles to ARM templates. KQL and SQL query data, while a DNS zone file describes name records.

references.title: Describe tools for managing and deploying Azure resources

items.prompt: Before signing in, an operator wants a broad view of major Azure platform incidents worldwide. Which page should be checked?
items.explanation: Azure status gives a public global view of service availability. After sign-in, Service Health offers more personalized impact information for the organization’s subscriptions.

items.prompt: A dashboard must combine charts of platform metrics with results from log queries. Which service is the common monitoring platform?
items.explanation: Azure Monitor collects and visualizes metrics and logs and supports dashboards, workbooks, queries, and alerts. Files stores data, Policy governs state, and tags provide metadata.

items.prompt: Which component defines who receives an email or which automation runs when an Azure Monitor alert fires?
items.explanation: An action group is the reusable collection of notification and automation endpoints invoked by Azure Monitor alerts. The other group types organize governance, resources, or database availability.

items.prompt: A web request calls two downstream services, and developers need to follow the operation across all dependencies. What telemetry is most useful?
items.explanation: Application Insights correlates requests and dependency telemetry so a transaction can be traced through distributed components. Financial, governance, and deletion controls do not record application call paths.
